name: Can siding work be done during rainy weather in coastal cities?

mainEntity.name: What happens if it rains while siding is being replaced in Pacifica or Daly City?
mainEntity.text: What happens if it rains while siding is being replaced in Pacifica or Daly City?

acceptedAnswer.text: Rain is a major scheduling issue for siding work in coastal Bay Area cities. If the wall is open, the contractor must protect the structure from water intrusion before stopping for the day, and they should avoid exposing sheathing or insulation to prolonged moisture. Light drizzle may be manageable for some exterior tasks, but active rain and wind usually create unacceptable risk. Homeowners should ask how the crew handles unexpected weather, what temporary coverings are used, and whether the project is planned in phases so the house is never left vulnerable overnight. This matters especially for tear-off jobs, where the old siding comes off before the new system is ready. A responsible contractor will explain the weather plan in advance rather than improvising on site.
acceptedAnswer.description: Siding installation can sometimes continue in light conditions, but active rain and high winds often require stopping work or protecting exposed walls. A good contractor should keep the building weather-tight each day and avoid leaving sheathing or insulation exposed to moisture.
